info.title: Streaming API
info.description: Music streaming backend for a Media3/ExoPlayer client. Streaming works in two steps: request a short-lived signed URL via `/songs/{id}/stream-url`, then feed that URL to the player. The `/stream/{songId}` endpoint fully supports HTTP Range requests (200 / 206 / 416), which ExoPlayer relies on for seeking.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Phone-number authentication with a mocked OTP and JWT access/refresh tokens.

tags.name: me
tags.description: Endpoints scoped to the signed-in user (require a Bearer access token).

tags.name: memberships
tags.description: Premium plans and purchase (mock card payment). The price catalog is public; checkout requires a Bearer access token.

tags.name: playback
tags.description: Server-authoritative "what do I play next?". Free users get an ad once every 3 songs; premium users never do.

post.summary: Request an OTP for a phone number (mock)
post.description: Starts the login/registration flow. **No SMS is actually sent** — this is a mock. The response includes `firstTime`: it is `true` when the phone has no account yet, or has an account that has not finished registration (first name, last name and birth date not all filled in). When `firstTime` is `true`, send the user through `POST /api/v1/me/update-informations` after verifying. Follow up with `POST /auth/otp/verify`.

phone.description: Phone number in E.164-ish form (a leading + is kept).

firstTime.description: True when the phone is new or registration is incomplete.

post.summary: Verify an OTP and obtain access + refresh tokens
post.description:
Verifies the OTP for a phone number. Because the SMS system is mocked, the following codes are **ALWAYS valid**:

- `280600`
- `260702`
- `250506`
- `101000`
- `346134`
- `123456`

Any other code returns **401**. On success the user is created on first login (passwordless signup) and a JWT access token plus an opaque refresh token are returned. Send the access token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` to the `/api/v1/me/*` endpoints.

code.description: One of the always-valid mock codes listed above.

post.summary: Exchange a refresh token for a new token pair (rotation)
post.description: Validates the refresh token, then **rotates** it: the presented token is revoked and a brand-new access + refresh pair is returned. A revoked, expired or unknown token returns 401.
Revokes the given refresh token so it can no longer be used to refresh. Idempotent — revoking an already-revoked or unknown token still returns 200.

Sets the user's first name, last name and birth date — the registration step for `firstTime` users. Once all three are set the user's `profileCompleted` becomes true and subsequent `otp/request` calls return `firstTime: false`. Can also be used later to edit the profile.

birthDate.description: Calendar date in YYYY-MM-DD; must be a valid, non-future date.

post.description: Records that the user played a song. This is what powers "Son Çalınanlar" (recently played) and the recommendation endpoints. The client should call this once per real playback (not per Range request).

get.description: Distinct songs the user has played, ordered by the most recent play of each.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of songs to return.

get.description: A mix drawn from the user's most-played artists. Falls back to the newest catalog when the user has no play history yet.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of songs to return.

get.description: More songs by artists the user has played (already-played songs are included). Falls back to the newest catalog when there is no history.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of songs to return.

delete.summary: Delete one of the user's own playlists
delete.description: Deletes the playlist and all of its tracks. The caller must own it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Playlist id (must be owned by the caller).

403.description: You do not own this playlist

post.summary: Add a song to one of the user's playlists

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Playlist id (must be owned by the caller).

403.description: You do not own this playlist

409.description: Song is already in the playlist

delete.summary: Remove a song from one of the user's playlists
delete.description: Removes the song and resequences the remaining tracks so positions stay contiguous.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Playlist id (must be owned by the caller).

403.description: You do not own this playlist

404.description: Playlist not found or song not in the playlist

get.description: Lists the purchasable premium plans. **recurring** auto-renews monthly at 139₺; **one-time** is a single 30-day month at 159₺ with no renewal. Prices are also given in kuruş (1₺ = 100 kuruş).

post.summary: Buy a premium membership (mock card payment)
post.description:
Charges a card (mock — no real payment provider) and, on approval, activates a premium membership for the calling user. The outcome is decided entirely by the card number. **Test cards:**

| Card number | Result |
| --- | --- |
| `4242 4242 4242 4242` | ✅ Approved |
| `4000 0000 0000 0002` | ❌ Declined (402) |

Any other card number is also declined. Pick the plan with `plan`: `recurring` (139₺/ay) or `one-time` (159₺).

post.summary: Resolve the next playable item (song, or an ad first)
post.description:
Call this right before playing a song instead of minting a stream URL directly. The server records the play and decides what to serve:

- **Premium users** always get `type: "song"`.
- **Free users** get `type: "ad"` once every 3 songs (counter kept server-side). The ad response **also** includes the song, so the client queues `[ad, song]`; after the ad call `/playback/ad-complete`.

Because this records the play, clients using it should **not** also call `POST /api/v1/me/plays`.

200.description: Either a song to play, or an ad followed by the song

post.summary: Mark a served ad as watched through
post.description: Report that the ad from a `type: "ad"` response finished playing. Records the impression as completed (analytics). Pass the `impressionId` from that response.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response (`nextCursor`). Omit for the first page.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Case-insensitive search over title, artist and album.

nextCursor.description: Pass back as `cursor` to fetch the next page; null when exhausted.

400.description: Invalid limit, cursor or search query

get.summary: Mint a short-lived signed stream URL (premium only)
get.description:
Returns a URL the client passes directly to ExoPlayer. The signature is an HMAC-SHA256 over `<songId>:<expiresAt>`; TTL is 300 seconds by default. Mint right before playback rather than caching alongside list responses.

**Premium only.** This bypasses ad gating, so free-tier users get 403 and must use `POST /api/v1/me/playback/next` instead.

Serves the audio object referenced by a signed URL. Single byte ranges are honored (`bytes=N-`, `bytes=N-M`, `bytes=-N`); multi-range or unknown units fall back to the full body, as RFC 9110 permits. With an S3/CloudFront storage provider this endpoint responds 302 to a CDN-signed URL instead of proxying.

parameters.name: expires
parameters.description: Expiry as Unix epoch seconds (part of the signed token).

parameters.name: Range
parameters.description: Standard HTTP byte range; ExoPlayer sends this on seek.

206.description: Partial content for a satisfiable Range

302.description: Redirect to a CDN-signed URL (only with an external storage provider)

404.description: Song or audio object not found

200.description: Playlist with its songs in track order

bearerAuth.description: Paste the `accessToken` from `POST /api/v1/auth/otp/verify`. It is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

PlaybackSong.description: Play the song directly (premium, or no ad due yet).

PlaybackAd.description: Play the ad first, then the included song.

impressionId.description: Pass to /playback/ad-complete once the ad finishes.

ownerId.description: Owner user id, or null for public/"featured" catalog playlists.

profileCompleted.description: True once firstName, lastName and birthDate are all set.

membership.description: The active premium membership (type + expiry), or null on the free tier. Returned by login (`/auth/otp/verify`) and `GET /api/v1/me`.

expiresAt.description: Sona erme tarihi — when premium access ends.

accessToken.description: Short-lived JWT (HS256). Send as a Bearer token.

refreshToken.description: Opaque, long-lived token. Exchange via /auth/refresh; rotated on use.

expiresIn.description: Seconds until the access token expires.

firstTime.description: True when registration is not yet complete (collect profile via /api/v1/me/update-informations).
